visual_features.overall_style: Clean, modern, minimalist editorial layout with strong reliance on white space, thin rules, and a restrained monochrome palette. Feels like a contemporary design studio / magazine rather than a typical e‑commerce site.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is driven by typographic scale, contrast, and spatial grouping. The logo in the top-left (bold 'FAB' vs lighter 'BRICA.') is the first focal point, followed by the centered top navigation. Section titles such as 'About admin' and the article title 'OPENING' are bold and slightly larger, creating clear entry points into content. The large feature image in the article card forms a strong secondary focal point. Thin horizontal rules separate semantic blocks (author info, article card, meta/footer), reinforcing hierarchy through segmentation.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Very generous white space. Large top and side margins frame a centered content column. Ample vertical spacing between the header, author block, article block, and meta row creates an airy, editorial feel. Individual text elements within a block are more tightly grouped, but blocks themselves are separated by substantial whitespace and thin dividing lines for clear scannability.
unique_elements: Logo treatment where the brand name is split into a bold black segment ('FAB') and a lighter gray segment ('BRICA.') for visual contrast within the wordmark.
Top navigation divided into evenly-sized vertical segments using ultra-thin vertical rules, creating a grid-like, almost menu-bar feel.
Author block with a generic circular avatar icon and an inline mail icon aligned with a horizontal divider, emphasizing a minimalist profile / contact row.
Article listing styled as a horizontal media object (large image left, text right) with a full-width horizontal rule and right-aligned 'Read More' link.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Visually consistent with a centered fixed-width layout on a 12-column grid (approx. 1100–1200px content width) with generous outer margins. Content within each major section appears to follow a simple 2-column grid (image vs text) for article listings.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Single-column, centered layout with full-width header/navigation and stacked content sections. Within the main content column, the article listing uses a horizontal split (image left, copy right).
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top horizontal navigation bar with logo on the far left, navigation items centered in evenly divided cells, and utilities (language toggle 'EN' and search icon) on the right. Below this: an 'About admin' author info block, followed by a separated article list section containing the feature article card, then a meta row (date, category tag, right-aligned 'Read More' link). Sections are separated using horizontal rules and significant vertical spacing.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Strong left alignment for all content text and imagery within the main column. The navigation items are center-aligned within their individual cells along the horizontal axis. Meta details like date/category are left-aligned while the 'Read More' call to action is right-aligned in the same row, creating a balanced tension. Vertical alignment uses consistent baselines across items within each row (e.g., image top aligns with article title top).
responsive_hints: The clear two-column article card (image and text) suggests a breakpoint where these would stack vertically on smaller screens.
Navigation items are evenly distributed and could collapse into a hamburger menu on mobile, though not visible here.
The centered main column with large side margins indicates the design will adapt by reducing side margins on smaller viewports.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: (1) Full-width logo + navigation bar at the top. (2) A single author information block spanning the main content width, with avatar on the left and text to the right. (3) A wide separation line, then the primary article card: a large landscape image block aligned on the left occupying roughly one-third of the content width, and to its right a text column with article title, summary paragraph, and teaser ellipsis. (4) Immediately below the card, a slim horizontal rule with a meta row: publication date and category on the left, 'Read More >' inline link on the right.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Header/navigation consumes the full width but relatively small vertical height due to slim typography and minimal padding. The main content column likely occupies ~60–70% of the viewport width and is centered with comfortable side margins. Inside the article card, the image occupies about 35–40% of the horizontal space and the text column about 60–65%. Vertical spacing between major blocks (header, author section, article card, meta line) is generous (roughly 32–48px), while spacing within blocks (title to paragraph, paragraph to rules) is moderate (12–20px).
layout_characteristics.content_density: Spacious editorial layout with ample breathing room and low information density. Only a handful of elements are visible at once: one author block and one article card with meta. Each has clear separation and minimal embellishment. The density appears deliberately light to emphasize brand and imagery rather than a high volume of products or posts.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Eye flow follows a mild Z-pattern: beginning at the bold logo on the top-left, scanning right across the navigation, then dropping down to the 'About admin' heading and avatar. From there the eye moves downward to the article image (strong visual anchor) on the left, then across to the article title 'OPENING' and summary text on the right. Finally, the flow drops to the bottom meta row, scanning date/category on the left to the 'Read More >' link on the far right.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Primary interactions in the screenshot appear as textual links (e.g., 'Read More >') rather than traditional filled buttons. These links are rendered as simple text in a dark gray/black color, likely with no background fill, no visible border, and possibly a subtle underline on hover. Corners are therefore not a factor; elements maintain a flat, typographic look. The visual emphasis comes from contrast and proximity rather than decoration.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Buttons/CTAs are likely implemented as anchor tags (<a>) styled as textual links, sometimes with appended icons or carets (e.g., '>') to suggest direction. For a more prominent CTA elsewhere in the site, a minimal rectangular button with 2–4px radius, 1px border and subtle padding would be consistent, still likely using <a> elements styled via CSS rather than <button> tags.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Article entries function as cards but with very minimal visual framing. Each 'card' consists of an image on the left and text on the right, with no visible shadow and no distinct bounding box except for thin horizontal rules above and below. Corners are sharp (0px radius). Internal padding is generous: the image and text column have clear spacing, and text has moderate padding from the card edges. The card feels flat and editorial rather than widget-like.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No full form is visible in this screenshot, but the minimal, flat aesthetic suggests form elements would be simple, with light 1px gray borders (#CCCCCC–#DDDDDD), no inner shadows, and slight padding (6–10px). Labels would likely sit above inputs, left-aligned, using a smaller gray type. Error or validation messages would probably appear as small colored text (red for error) beneath inputs with minimal iconography.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: A likely pattern is stacked, full-width inputs inside a vertical form (<form> with <div class='form-group'> wrappers), each containing a <label> above an <input> or <textarea>. Validation messages can be additional <small> elements beneath inputs, and grouped sections might be separated by horizontal rules following the rest of the design language.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Horizontal top navigation bar spanning the full width. Logo on the far left in bold black/gray, followed by navigation items (LIVING, CONTRACT, ABOUT US, NEWS, a star icon, CONTACT, EN, and a search icon) arranged in evenly sized segments. Items are uppercase, narrow letterspacing, and likely center-aligned vertically and horizontally within their segments. Vertical dividers (thin #EEEEEE lines) separate items. The active or special item (star icon) may be indicated via filled icon or slightly darker state, but still very minimal—no heavy backgrounds.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Markup is probably a <header> containing a logo anchor and a <nav> with an unordered list (<ul><li><a> pattern). Icon items (star, search) may use inline SVGs or icon fonts inside <a> tags. The equal-width segmentation is achieved through flexbox or table-like CSS (display: flex; justify-content: center; with each li flex: 1 for the central menu region).

ui_patterns.visual_description: Blog posts are presented as a vertically stacked list of media objects. Each item is separated with generous whitespace and a thin horizontal rule. Items are low density: a single large thumbnail (image) and a concise block of text (title and short excerpt). No bullet points or numbered markers; hierarchy relies on size and boldness of the title vs lighter excerpt. For meta information lists (date | category), items are inline separated by a thin vertical bar.

heading.visual_style: Bold geometric/neo-grotesque sans-serif with clean straight terminals and relatively high x-height. Uppercase headings (e.g., 'OPENING') are tightly spaced and visually strong but not overly condensed.

body.visual_style: Neutral, highly readable sans-serif with modest contrast and a modern feel, suitable for paragraphs of text and small meta descriptions.

spacing_system.component_padding: Typical component (e.g., cards, author block) padding appears around 16–24px inside the main container, with 8–12px between text lines within that component.
spacing_system.section_margin: Major sections (header to author block, author block to article list, article list to meta row) use approximately 32–48px vertical spacing, often reinforced by a thin horizontal rule.

interaction_hints: Navigation items likely change color (slightly darker/lighter) or show an underline on hover, while maintaining the minimalist aesthetic; active items may use a bolder text weight or the star icon filled state.
Text links such as 'Read More >' likely underline or shift color on hover, and may include a subtle rightward movement micro-interaction of the arrow/caret.
Image thumbnails in article cards may have a gentle zoom-in or brightness increase on hover, reflecting modern editorial interaction patterns.
Transitions between states (hover, focus) are likely subtle and fast (150–250ms) using CSS opacity or transform transitions, maintaining the clean and unobtrusive feel of the site.
